headline: Lynas and the Question Kuantan Never Got to Answer
context: Lynas Malaysia operates the world's largest rare earth processing plant in Gebeng, Kuantan — its licence has been renewed repeatedly despite unresolved waste disposal.

cards.big: World's largest rare earth refinery. Waste plan: still pending.
cards.sub: Lynas has operated in Kuantan since 2012. Permanent disposal facility: not built.

cards.big: Over 1.5 million metric tonnes of WLP residue stored on site.
cards.sub: Radioactivity levels are low but the volume is unprecedented and growing.

cards.big: Lynas contributes RM300M annually and 3,000 jobs to Pahang.
cards.sub: Economic dependency makes regulatory enforcement politically costly.

cards.big: Licence conditions changed three times in five years.
cards.sub: 2019: build PDF by 2023. 2022: extended to 2025. 2024: extended again.

cards.big: When a condition is extended every time it comes due, is it a condition?
cards.sub: Regulation without enforcement is permission with extra paperwork.

cards.big: Rare earths matter strategically. So does enforcing the rules. Malaysia should not have to choose.
